title: The Art of True Crime Documentary with Erin Lee Carr | The Big Picture (Ep. 46)
format: Documentarian Erin Lee Carr on chasing morally ambiguous true-crime stories, from the Cannibal Cop to Valeant, and what she learned from her father David Carr.

films.title: Thought Crimes
films.note: Her HBO debut, made at 26, about the so-called Cannibal Cop.

films.title: Mommy Dead and Dearest
films.note: Her follow-up HBO film, on a Munchausen by proxy case.

films.title: Capturing the Friedmans
films.note: The Andrew Jarecki film she cites as an early formative watch.

films.title: Page One: Inside the New York Times
films.note: Andrew Rossi's documentary; Rossi later told her to go make her own films.

films.title: Enron: The Smartest Guys in the Room
films.note: The Alex Gibney film she says she rewatched a dozen-plus times before joining his Dirty Money anthology.

segments.heading: The last great thing she's seen

films.title: Icarus
films.note: One of the Oscar-nominated docs she name-checks.

films.title: Joan Didion: The Center Will Not Hold
films.note: Directed by Didion's nephew Griffin Dunne; Carr calls it intimate, like listening in on a conversation.
